The model favours Brazil at 64%, with Scotland's win probability at 11%. The underdog thus faces a significant deficit, though not an insurmountable one in a single match.

The draw sits at 24%, suggesting a plausible stalemate outcome. Brazil's advantage is material—roughly 53 percentage points over Scotland—but the three-way split indicates this is not a heavily lopsided fixture. Competitive resistance from Scotland remains a live possibility within the forecast range.

Model and sportsbook are closely aligned across all three outcomes. Brazil's modelled win probability stands 4 percentage points below the consensus 68%, while the draw is 4 points higher in the model. These gaps are minor and reflect normal variance across forecasting methods rather than material disagreement. No Polymarket data is yet available for this fixture.
